feed.title: Combined summary - Fountain Codes: a way to reduce blockchain storage costs

entry.title: Combined summary - Fountain Codes: a way to reduce blockchain storage costs
entry.summary: Fountain Codes are gaining prominence as a transformative solution for managing Bitcoin's blockchain data, particularly through their capability to divide the blockchain into encoded segments known as droplets. This technology allows nodes to reconstruct the entire blockchain with only a subset of these droplets, significantly reducing individual storage needs. The research in collaboration with Vinteum elucidates the potential of Fountain Codes to enhance the Bitcoin network's efficiency and scalability. Interested readers can delve deeper into the nuances of this application and its benefits on the blockchain by visiting [this detailed blog post](https://lucasdbr05.com/posts/fountain-codes/). The method's technical foundation lies in the deterministic composition of droplets using a seed based on the peer's identity and block height, optimizing data storage and ensuring unbiased, random droplet selection. With parameters such as $k=1,000$ (total blocks) and $s=10$ (segments), this strategy demonstrates substantial storage savings, requiring just a fraction of the space traditionally needed. This approach also suggests economic efficiencies, potentially reducing disk space usage drastically, thus fostering more widespread availability of blockchain copies for download despite minimal immediate improvements in network availability. Conversely, the discussion also explores the substitution of fountain codes with Reed-Solomon (RS) codes due to security concerns in environments where peer reliability is questionable. RS codes provide robust solutions against malicious activities by enabling polynomial-time decoding, crucial for error correction from reliable peers. This choice is also influenced by the need to maintain node anonymity, preventing fingerprinting when nodes switch network identities—an essential aspect for preserving privacy within the network. Additional insights into the decoding speeds and practical applications of RS codes in non-hostile settings can be explored at the [GitHub repository for leopard](https://github.com/catid/leopard/). Furthermore, the strategic use of RS codes in creating a robust mechanism against malicious peers is highlighted. The technique involves the use of droplets containing XOR combinations of blocks, validated through the header chain to confirm single-block droplets and simplify the decoding of multi-block combinations. This ensures integrity across transactions and facilitates straightforward penalties for compromised nodes while maintaining node anonymity and network security. The structured approach to storing data across segmented groups based on block height enhances the scalability and efficiency of data management throughout the Bitcoin network. For those interested in a deeper dive into these methodologies, a comprehensive framework is available in the [2019 paper](https://arxiv.org/pdf/1906.12140). Lastly, the intricate strategies for managing anonymity groups within distributed networks emphasize the shift from targeting individual groups to compromising multiple groups to disrupt or control services effectively. This approach, coupled with the innovative encoding methods and detailed understanding of group dynamics and node functionalities within the network, underscores the complexity and sophistication required in contemporary network security measures and distributed system designs.
